factors.name: US-Iran Deal (Hormuz Strait Reopening)
factors.summary: Trump announced US-Iran ceasefire with Strait of Hormuz reopening by Friday, easing geopolitical risk premium. Al Jazeera reported S&P 500 +1.7%, Nasdaq +3.1% on initial deal euphoria, reducing oil war premium.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: Goldman Sachs cut Brent forecasts to $80 (2026) and $75 (2027); Morgan Stanley also cut forecasts materially. Lower energy costs reduce inflation pressure but hurt CVX (Dow component) earnings and energy sector.

factors.name: China Economic Deterioration
factors.summary: China retail sales fell -0.6% YoY (first decline since pandemic), FAI contracted -4.1% YTD vs -2% expected. Industrial output beat at +4.5%, but consumer weakness signals demand erosion, pressuring CAT, BA, and cyclical Dow names.

factors.name: BOJ Rate Hike to 1% (delivered)
factors.summary: BOJ raised to 1% as expected; yen initially pared gains vs USD. Bond taper pause (April 2027) seen as dovish structural element, limiting long-end yield pressure. Fed decision pending Wed; BoJ move already priced.

factors.name: Fed Policy Decision (Wednesday imminent)
factors.summary: FOMC decision due Wed evening. Markets now expect rate hold given softer inflation outlook from Iran deal and China weakness. Upcoming event within ~30h caps conviction; outcome will dominate Wed-Thu price action.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD Index holding near 99.70; initial Iran deal sparked brief risk-on (EUR/GBP strength), but USD reasserted. Dow has ~50% international revenue; sustained USD strength would pressurize FX translation for Dow heavyweights (MSFT, IBM, CAT).

factors.name: Real Yields / Treasury Long-End
factors.summary: 2-year Treasury dropped to 4.02% on Monday as rate-hike bets trimmed post-Iran deal. Lower nominal yields typically bullish equities, but if driven by growth concerns (China data) rather than Fed pivot, it signals recession risk for cyclical Dow.

factors.name: Dow Component Earnings Risk
factors.summary: No major Dow earnings releases in the past 24h. Next catalyst: forward guidance from CAT, BA, UNH, JPM on recession/demand outlook. China weakness and lower oil prices will weigh on energy (CVX) and industrials guidance.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment (VIX, Credit Spreads)
factors.summary: Iran ceasefire and Hormuz reopening sparked broad risk-on: S&P +1.7%, Nasdaq +3.1% reported. VIX likely compressed; emerging market equities and currencies outperforming vs safe-haven rotation. Dow benefiting from EM/cyclical rotation.

factors.name: Recent Price Action (Dow -0.48% 4h, -0.25% 24h)
factors.summary: Despite initial Iran deal euphoria late Monday (S&P +1.7%, Ndaq +3.1%), Dow underperforming and now in mild selloff. Suggests energy (CVX weight) weakness and China growth concerns outweighing geopolitical relief for Dow's industrial/cyclical tilt.

screening.narrative: The Dow faces a crosscurrent narrative: Iran ceasefire and Hormuz reopening (initial positive for risk sentiment, visible in S&P/Nasdaq +1.7–3.1%) collides with China economic deterioration (retail sales first decline since pandemic, FAI contraction) and sharp oil price cuts by Goldman/Morgan Stanley. Energy names (CVX) are being pressured by lower crude forecasts, while industrial cyclicals (CAT, BA) face headwinds from China demand weakness. The Fed decision Wednesday looms as the dominant 30h catalyst; market is pricing a hold with dovish bias, but any surprise hawkish hold or fresh Fed guidance could revert the recent rate-cut expectations. Dow's -0.48% 4h move suggests the energy drag and growth concerns are currently outweighing the geopolitical relief.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision + Powell presser (Wednesday ~19:00 UTC, ~14:00 ET) — expect focus on Iran deal inflation impact, China data, and Fed's forward guidance timing
Fed speakers (Barkin, Kashkari, others) post-decision may refine rate-cut expectations
China Caixin Services PMI (likely Wed or Thu) — follow-up to weak retail/FAI data
ISM Manufacturing PMI (likely Friday if on schedule) — direct read on Dow industrials health
Weekly initial jobless claims (Thursday) — household demand / labor market signal
Oil market developments (shipping insurance normalization, Iranian vessel flows via Hormuz) — real-time driver of CVX earnings and energy margin outlooks
screening.risk_warning: FOMC Wednesday could trigger sharp reversal if Fed signals faster rate cuts (bullish equities short-term, but bearish if driven by growth cuts) or if Powell dismisses Iran deal inflation relief as temporary. Conversely, hawkish hold or hawkish dots would reignite rate-hike bets, crushing the Iran-deal-driven lower-yield rally. China data deterioration (retail sales contraction, FAI miss) could worsen if next data point shows further softening, invalidating the cyclical-recovery assumption. Oil prices could stabilize above $80 (if Hormuz opening is slower than expected), reducing downside to CVX and supporting energy sector, which would lift Dow. Additionally, if the Iran MOU unravels or geopolitical tensions resume (Netanyahu signaling potential escalation in Middle East), risk-off could dominate, especially given Dow's industrial exposure.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (initial Iran euphoria unwinding into China/energy headwinds). Avoid chasing shorts into Wednesday FOMC; wait for Fed decision clarity and a potential pullback retest of 51,400–51,500 support to re-engage with lower risk/reward.

factors.name: Iran Peace Deal & Oil Collapse
factors.summary: US-Iran framework agreement signed; oil (WTI) crashed to 3-month low near $79.20 (per Bloomberg 06:43 UTC). Lower energy input costs and inflation expectations benefit cyclical industrials (CAT, HON, BA). However, move is already priced in (oil down 3%+ on the day).

factors.name: FOMC Decision Tomorrow (Wednesday)
factors.summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh expected to deliver 'slightly hawkish' shift in statement and dot plot per BNY preview (06:32 UTC). This contradicts the dovish oil-linked rally and creates execution risk into the decision. Major uncertainty ahead.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations & Real Yields
factors.summary: Despite lower oil prices, markets are repricing Fed hawkishness. Gold rally (up ~6.5% per FXStreet 06:24 UTC) signals real yield concerns remain elevated. Dividend-heavy Dow (utilities, REITs) at risk if nominal yields stay sticky.

factors.name: Global Central Bank Tightening Cycle
factors.summary: BoJ hiked to 1.0% (highest since 1995); RBA kept rates at 4.35% and signaled further hikes possible per Reuters. This global hawkish backdrop limits downside for USD and constrains equity multiple expansion.

factors.name: Earnings & Mega-Cap Momentum Divergence
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO surging (+11% premarket, set for 50%+ 3-session rally per Bloomberg 08:09 UTC) lifts sentiment, but core Dow industrials (BA, CAT, GE, MMM) lack forward guidance updates. No major earnings catalyst visible in next 24-48h.

factors.name: German Economic Sentiment & Europe Stabilization
factors.summary: German ZEW investor outlook jumped to +10.5 vs -6.0 expected (per Investinglive 09:00 UTC) on Iran resolution; current conditions weak at -81.0. Eases European recession fears, helps Dow multinational revenue outlook (BAX, MMM, CAT export demand).

factors.name: USD Strength Despite Oil Decline
factors.summary: DXY rallying despite WTI collapse per ING (08:15 UTC: 'Fed focus offsets oil slide'). Strong dollar headwinds translate to lower earnings for Dow's 50% international revenue exposure; CAT, BA, V most exposed.

factors.name: China Growth Stalling & AI Relief Fades
factors.summary: Bloomberg (07:11 UTC) notes 'AI Relief Rally Fades While Chinese Economy Stalls.' No fresh PMI data visible, but commentary suggests demand concerns persist. This matters for CAT, BA, MSFT cloud exposure.

factors.name: Treasury Yields & Duration Risk
factors.summary: Oil decline typically drives 10Y yield lower, but hawkish Fed expectations may offset. No specific yield data in news, but with FOMC tomorrow, bond market is pricing in only modest dovishness. 10Y likely stays 4.0-4.2% range.

factors.name: VIX & Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: No VIX data provided, but daily move (-0.25% in 24h, -0.48% in 4h) suggests orderly consolidation, not panic. Iran deal removed tail risk, but FOMC uncertainty keeps vol support in place.

factors.name: Price Action: Down 0.48% in 4h, -0.25% in 24h
factors.summary: Small selloff already in process; move is PHASE 2 (underway but not extended). Suggests market pricing in FOMC hawkishness and questioning whether oil relief is sustainable into peak Fed tightening.

screening.narrative: The Dow sold off modestly (-0.25% in 24h) despite a powerful bullish catalyst: US-Iran peace deal and oil crash to 3-month lows (~$79.20 WTI). Lower energy input costs should be unambiguously positive for cyclical industrials (CAT, MMM, HON). However, the rally has been muted because markets are simultaneously repricing the Federal Reserve for hawkishness. Chair Kevin Warsh delivers the FOMC decision tomorrow (Wednesday), with bank strategists signaling a 'slightly hawkish' shift in guidance. This contradicts the dovish implications of lower oil and easing inflation expectations, creating a classic 'good news priced as bad news' dynamic. Global central banks (BoJ, RBA, ECB likely next) are also tightening, limiting multiple expansion for dividend-payers and large-cap Dow names. The move is in PHASE 2: oil-driven relief is pricing in, but FOMC uncertainty is capping upside and keeping sellers engaged.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Decision & Statement (Wednesday, June 16–17, ~19:00 UTC) — Warsh's guidance and hawkish tone likely
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (if released early June — will show post-Iran demand pulse)
Initial Jobless Claims (weekly, if unrevised high, supports Fed hawkishness)
Dow component earnings (UNH, GS, JPM) — forward guidance on capex/buybacks amid higher rates
Oil price stabilization (watch for Hormuz reopening delays — could reverse rally)
USD strength/DXY continuation (headwind for multinational translation)
screening.risk_warning: This bias would flip BULLISH if: (1) FOMC proves dovish or signals pause; (2) oil extends collapse past $75, forcing real-yield expectations significantly lower; (3) Dow industrials (CAT, BA, GE) issue upbeat guidance citing lower input costs; (4) China PMI rebounds sharply. Flip to SELL if: (1) FOMC sounds hawkish enough to push 10Y above 4.3%; (2) DXY rallies past 106, strangling multinational earnings; (3) Energy costs rise again if Hormuz reopening stalls; (4) Earnings revisions roll over ahead of peak-rate scenario.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is PHASE 2 (underway but not extended). Avoid chasing sells into FOMC; instead, wait for a retest of 51,400–51,500 on any intra-day relief bounce. If FOMC proves dovish (surprise), buy the dip with target 52,000+. If FOMC is hawkish, expect breakdown toward 51,000; short only if 10Y breaks above 4.25%.

factors.name: Fed Policy Decision (FOMC Tomorrow)
factors.summary: Fed begins two-day meeting; expected to remove easing bias from statement but hold rates. High event risk ahead caps conviction; Kevin Warsh leadership signals 'no change for a while' per CNBC Fed Survey.

factors.name: US-Iran Deal & Geopolitical De-Escalation
factors.summary: Trump signals 99.9% of deal secured; Hormuz reopening expected Friday. Reduces tail risk, stabilizes energy complex and global supply chains—broadly constructive for industrials and cyclicals.

factors.name: Oil Collapse & Energy Input Cost Relief
factors.summary: WTI down $3.70 (4.6%) to $77, Brent below $80 for first time in 3+ months. Extends sharp 3-day selloff. Reduces input costs for transportation and manufacturing—positive for Dow's industrial heavy weighting.

factors.name: Housing Starts & Construction Weakness
factors.summary: May housing starts 1.177M vs 1.430M expected, down 15.4% m/m, hitting 8-month low. Single-family units slumped to 882K annualized. Signals demand weakness in cyclical consumer and construction sectors.

factors.name: Import Prices Surprise Hot
factors.summary: May import prices +1.9% vs +1.0% expected, +6.7% y/y. While energy tailwind helps, broader imported inflation still elevated; may support Fed caution despite oil drop.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Flat; No Bold Direction
factors.summary: 10-year yields 'little changed' ahead of FOMC; no decisive move. Absence of yield decline limits upside catalyst for dividend-heavy Dow; absence of spike avoids bearish pressure.

factors.name: Tech/Nasdaq Sector Reversal & Semiconductor Weakness
factors.summary: Nasdaq down 0.5% intraday; semis under pressure (NVDA -1.78%, Intel -5.7%, AMD down). SpaceX (17% gain day 3) gives back some strength. Dow less exposed to semis, but tech correlation shows risk-off tone.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Mania & AI Darling Strength
factors.summary: SpaceX briefly surpassed Microsoft; $60B Cursor deal announced. AI/growth narrative still alive; Rackspace, A10 Networks surging. Supports broad equity appetite despite Nasdaq chop.

factors.name: ADP Employment Cooling
factors.summary: 4-week average private-sector hiring fell to 25.5K (late May). Slowdown in labor demand precedes broader cyclical weakness; relevant to Dow's sensitivity to employment/consumer.

factors.name: Dollar Flat; USD/JPY Unmoved by BoJ Hike
factors.summary: EURUSD, USDJPY, GBPUSD all within 0.04% unchanged despite BoJ 25bp hike to 1.0%. USD resilience limits drag on multinationals but also limits tailwind; DXY static.

factors.name: Yum Brands Pizza Hut Divestiture
factors.summary: YUM selling Pizza Hut for $2.7B, refocusing on KFC and Taco Bell. Not in Dow, but shows consumer discretionary pruning; mixed signal (financial optimization vs. biz weakness).

factors.name: Security/Geopolitical Incident (FBI UFC Plot)
factors.summary: FBI disrupted alleged drone attack plot on White House UFC event. Contained; no market impact observed. Risk remains on radar but not priced in materially.

screening.narrative: The Dow faces a mixed setup 24h before the FOMC decision. US-Iran deal and oil collapse ($77 WTI) reduce geopolitical and energy input-cost risks, supporting the industrial and cyclical bias—core Dow strength. However, May housing starts collapsed (1.177M vs 1.430M expected, -15.4% m/m), import prices surprised hot (+1.9% vs +1.0%), and ADP employment cooled to 25.5K/week average, signaling demand weakness. Tech/Nasdaq also showing intraday reversal despite SpaceX euphoria. Treasury yields remain flat with no clear directional signal ahead of the Fed. Confidence remains capped by imminent FOMC event risk and conflicting data (relief from geopolitics vs. softening cyclicals).
screening.entry_guidance: Move already underway (oil down sharply, Dow breadth mixed). Wait for FOMC announcement tomorrow (post-14:00 ET) before committing; retest key support ~43,200–43,500 would be lower-risk entry if Fed removes easing bias without signaling cuts.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Decision & Statement (Wed 18:00 ET) — Expected to drop easing bias; major inflection point
Kevin Warsh (new Fed Chair) inaugural policy commentary — market re-pricing
Iran deal formal signing in Switzerland (Friday) — confirms Hormuz reopening timeline
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI data (late week) — directs cyclical and Dow exposure
Dow heavyweight earnings (UNH, JPM, GS, BA, CAT, HD, V) — individual names key to index
China PMI and growth data — impacts CAT, BA, materials demand
screening.risk_warning: FOMC hawkish surprise (holds longer than expected, raises rates, or removes 2024 cut guidance) would flip bias BEARISH and pressure Dow yields/valuations. Reversal of Iran deal or renewed Middle East tension would re-ignite oil and geopolitical risk premium. Worsening housing/labor data (NFP miss next month) could trigger recession fears and pull equities lower. Data dependency is high; positioning on either side of the Fed into tomorrow is high-risk.
